---
title: ""
page-layout: full
image: "headshot.jpeg"

about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-width: 65%
  image-shape: round

listing:
  - id: articles
    contents: 
      - research/articles
      # - posts/post-links.yml
    exclude:
      title: ""
    type: grid
    max-items: 3
#   image-height: 150px
    page-size: 3 
    fields: [date, title, subtitle, author]
    max-description-length: 75
    sort: 
      - "date desc"
      - "title asc"
---

::: {#hero-heading}
### About me

I am an assistant professor at [Texas State University](https://www.txst.edu), studying policing at the intersection of technology, innovation, and public health. My work examines how emerging tools — from AI-assisted body-worn cameras to de-escalation training — shape police behavior and community outcomes.

::: {.research-chips}
[Policing]{.chip} [Technology & AI]{.chip} [De-escalation]{.chip} [Use of Force]{.chip} [Public Health]{.chip} [Body-Worn Cameras]{.chip}
:::
:::

### Recent activity

::: {#articles}
:::

::: {style="text-align: right;"}
[All publications »](research.qmd)
:::
